tags.description: The unusual Bell Building on Lambeth Road: an early 20th century neo-Tudor building that replaced the Bell Inn, on the site since 1560. Its name is thought to come from the bell that would summon ferryman to their boats at the river here.
tags.name: The Bell Building

tags.description: In the shape of a beetle, gnarled legs as seats. Hand-carved. The roof is red cedar and the seats and supports have been carved from a single oak tree. The bower also features a mosaic created by local social enterprise Southbank Mosaics.
tags.name: Arthur’s Oak Bower
